status_logic: If user reports food insecurity, reduced income, no income, high rent relative to income, or household with children/seniors/disability: status = likely_worth_checking.
If location is outside California: status = local_handoff_recommended and route to USA.gov/USDA state directory, not CA sources.
If urgent food need today: include food-bank/211 immediate handoff before application prep.
never_claim: You will receive a specific dollar amount
prep_questions: Which county do you live in or spend most nights in?
How many people buy and prepare food together?
What is your approximate monthly income range?
Do you have rent, utilities, medical, child care, or dependent-care costs?
Do you need food today, before any application is processed?
documents_to_prepare: Proof of income or reduced hours if available

status_logic: If user is pregnant, recently pregnant, breastfeeding/chestfeeding, caring for an infant, or has a child under 5: status = likely_worth_checking.
If household includes children but ages are unknown: status = needs_more_information.
If no pregnancy/postpartum/breastfeeding and no child under 5 indicated: status = not_enough_evidence.
never_claim: Your local WIC office will accept your documents
prep_questions: Is anyone pregnant, recently pregnant, breastfeeding/chestfeeding, or caring for a child under age 5?
What are the child ages?
What city/ZIP should be used for WIC office search?
Do you prefer phone, text, or in-person contact?
documents_to_prepare: Proof of California residence/address if available
Income information if asked by WIC office

display_name: Health coverage: Medi-Cal / children’s coverage / Covered California
status_logic: If user lacks health coverage, has children, has reduced work hours, lost job, or needs low-cost health coverage: status = likely_worth_checking.
If user already has Medi-Cal but reports address/income/family change: status = local_handoff_recommended for county update/renewal.
If health coverage question is generic: provide educational content from HealthCare.gov and California apply links.
never_claim: You should choose a specific plan
prep_questions: Do you currently have health insurance?
Are you applying for yourself, children, or both?
Has your income, address, household, pregnancy status, or other coverage changed recently?
Which county should your application be routed to?
documents_to_prepare: Income or reduced-hours proof if available
Current health coverage notices if any
County/BenefitsCal login details kept by the user only, not entered into BenefitBridge

display_name: Utilities and phone help: LIHEAP / HEAP / California LifeLine
status_logic: If user reports utility bill difficulty, shutoff notice, high energy burden, heating/cooling need, or no phone service: status = likely_worth_checking.
If user reports no utility account because unhoused: status = local_handoff_recommended for 211/shelter/county resources and LifeLine if phone need exists.
If user has phone/internet affordability issue: include California LifeLine as a separate path.
never_claim: Your energy bill will be paid
prep_questions: Are you behind on an energy bill or facing shutoff?
Do you have a utility bill or shutoff notice?
Is the utility account in your name?
Do you need help keeping phone service active?
documents_to_prepare: Recent utility bill or shutoff notice if available
Proof of income or benefit participation if available
Phone provider/account information kept by user only

display_name: Housing help: shelter, homelessness, rental help, housing counseling
status_logic: If user reports sleeping outside/in car/unsafe tonight: immediate_handoff first, not normal benefit-prep flow.
If Santa Clara/San José and shelter needed: route to Here4You 408-385-2400 and 211.
If San Francisco and homelessness risk: route to Access Points / Adult Shelter Access Line / 311/211 style handoff from official sources.
If eviction or legal papers: route to legal aid, not benefits advice.
If general housing counseling: use HUD Housing Counselor API/tool.
If county-specific shelter/rental resources are not curated yet: route to 211 or official county social services and do not claim live availability.
never_claim: There is a shelter bed available
You are eligible for rental assistance
prep_questions: Where are you tonight: housed, doubled up, car/RV, shelter, outside, or unsafe?
Are there children, youth, seniors, disability, pregnancy, or domestic violence safety concerns?
Have you received eviction papers or a notice to quit?
What city/county should be used for resource routing?
Do you have a safe phone number or preferred contact method?
documents_to_prepare: Lease/rent notice or eviction papers if applicable
Any prior case/resource numbers kept by user only

display_name: Cash / county social services: CalWORKs, GA, CAAP, CAPI/refugee/trafficking-victim paths
status_logic: If household has children and needs cash aid: CalWORKs may be worth checking; route to BenefitsCal/county office.
If Santa Clara adult without dependent children and no other means of support: GA may be worth checking.
If San Francisco adult without dependent children: CAAP may be worth checking.
If immigrant/senior/disability/refugee/trafficking facts arise: do not advise; route to county social services/legal aid.
never_claim: You will receive a monthly amount
prep_questions: Are there dependent children in your household?
Are you applying as an adult without dependent children?
Which county do you live in?
Do you want to discuss immigration-related program questions with an agency or legal-aid provider?
documents_to_prepare: Proof of income/no income if available
